response.justification: Strong backend engineer with high-scale distributed-systems experience and current agentic-AI exposure, but critically lacks executive team-scaling track record (no evidence of building 10-30+ person teams, which is an explicit requirement) and production-depth agentic-AI systems experience. Fintech exposure is limited to one brief role; no demonstrated compliance or regulatory infrastructure experience. Better positioned for principal engineer or VP engineering roles than CTO.
strengths: Proven high-scale distributed systems engineering (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby, event-driven microservices architecture)
Strong software craftsmanship foundation with TDD, CI/CD, testing rigor, and multi-language versatility across embedded, backend, and frontend systems
Current hands-on experience with agentic frameworks and AI agent infrastructure at re:cinq, showing active engagement with the space
concerns: No demonstrated experience scaling engineering teams—all roles appear to be individual contributor senior engineer positions; explicit requirement is building and leading 10-30+ person teams with internal technical leadership
Agentic AI and LLM production experience is very recent (started April 2025); no evidence of depth in LLM selection, fine-tuning, evaluation, multi-agent orchestration, or operational failure modes
Limited fintech-infrastructure depth (one investment-management role briefly) and no evidence of working in regulated financial environments with compliance, auditability, or credit-risk systems
